inputs.userGoal: Build a secure ML training pipeline with data stored in Azure Data Lake, enable audit logging for all training jobs, and encrypt all outputs.

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 as the canonical training data and output store with encryption-at-rest and hardened defaults.
reasoning.tradeoffs: ADLS Gen2 adds ACL and analytics flexibility but introduces slightly more operational complexity than simple blob storage.

references.title: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 introduction

reasoning.whyChosen: Central log sink for auditability and operational telemetry for all training jobs and related resources.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Log Analytics improves queryability and alerting, but incurs ingestion and retention costs.

reasoning.whyChosen: Stores secrets, credentials, and future key material used by ML workspace components under least-privilege access.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds a managed dependency but improves separation of duties and secret governance.

resources.name: ai-mltrain-eus-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Supports end-to-end observability for ML workflows and workspace-level diagnostics.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds telemetry richness and troubleshooting depth at added ingestion cost.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace.

reasoning.whyChosen: Hosts training environment images with secure, private-by-default access posture.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU adds cost but supports stronger reliability and feature headroom.

resources.name: mlw-traineus-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure ML workspace is the managed control plane for secure, repeatable training jobs integrated with ADLS data and logging.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Managed workspace simplifies governance and operations, but some networking and compute controls require additional configuration.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace depends on connected storage, secrets, telemetry, and container image registry.

resources.name: amlcpu-train-eus-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Managed training cluster with autoscaling and no public node IPs to reduce attack surface.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Autoscale controls cost, but cold starts can increase job startup latency.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Compute target is provisioned inside the AML workspace.

references.title: Set up compute targets for model training

resources.name: diag-mlw-audit-eus-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Captures all AML workspace and training job audit logs into Log Analytics for compliance and forensics.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Centralized diagnostics improve traceability but increase monitoring costs.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Diagnostic settings require both source resource and destination workspace.

overallReasoning.summary: This design uses Azure Machine Learning for managed training orchestration, ADLS Gen2 for secure data and encrypted outputs, and centralized Azure Monitor logging for mandatory audit trails.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Default SKUs reduce complexity and accelerate deployment, but may need right-sizing, private endpoint expansion, and CMK adoption for stricter compliance regimes.

plan.validation: All required capabilities are covered: secure data lake storage, encrypted output at rest and in transit, and full training-job audit logging routed to a centralized workspace.

references.title: Security baseline for Azure Machine Learning
